data.content: The bug is on line 29 — `ProcessPayment` checks for zero but not negative amounts. The fastest workflow is `dotnet watch test`, which hot-reloads and re-runs tests on every file save. Let me set it up:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet watch test to see initial failure

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet watch test to see initial failure

data.reasoningText:
The bug is clear: `ProcessPayment` validates zero but not negative amounts. The user wants the fastest iteration workflow. The best approach is `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests on every file save without a full rebuild.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode, then fix the bug, and show the user the workflow.

arguments.description: Run dotnet watch test to see initial failure

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-efd0e59697fd4b98ae976525cf6603e2 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet watch test to see initial failure", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content: Good — `dotnet watch` is running and waiting for changes. There are two issues: a missing `using System;` and the actual negative-amount validation. Let me fix both:

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
arguments.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

data.content: All 3 tests pass now. Let me stop the watcher:

Here's the summary:

**The fix:** Changed `amount == 0` to `amount <= 0` (line 29) and added the missing `using System;`.

**The fast-iteration workflow:** Run `dotnet watch test` — it monitors your files and automatically rebuilds + re-runs tests on every save. No manual rebuild needed. You just edit, save, and see results instantly.

To use it yourself going forward:

```bash
dotnet watch test --filter "ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException"
```

The `--filter` flag limits re-runs to just the test you're iterating on, making the feedback loop even faster.
